description_long: This house features a steeply pitched gable roof with red-brown shingles and a prominent brick chimney on one side. The exterior walls are characterized by a half-timbered design, with exposed wooden beams set against white infill panels, evoking a traditional Tudor architectural style. The front facade includes multiple vertically aligned windows and a ground-level entryway with glass panels, suggesting a main entrance for residents. The rear side mirrors the half-timbered pattern but is dominated by the large chimney and lacks visible doors. The side elevations are simpler, with the timber framing continuing uninterrupted and no windows or doors present. The overall structure is tall and narrow, with four visible stories and a robust, historic appearance suitable for a dense urban neighborhood.
description: A tall, narrow Tudor-style house with a steep gable roof, half-timbered exterior, and a prominent brick chimney. The facade features multiple windows and a glass-paneled entryway.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance with glass panels, vertically aligned windows, and the steep gable roof.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the uninterrupted half-timbered wall and the full expanse of the tiled roof, with the chimney just visible.
description_view_2: Rear view highlighting the large brick chimney, symmetrical window arrangement, and absence of a rear door.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with the half-timbered wall and the chimney running up the center, no windows or doors visible.
attribute_entryway_design: Main entryway with glass-paneled door, no porch, and a simple overhang integrated into the timber framing.
